name: User Proxy Agent

architecture.model: RAG + three-tier preference store + Platt-calibrated confidence + consequence weighting

decision_mechanism.trigger: Automatic based on calibrated confidence + consequence weight

advisor.behavior: NEVER auto-execute. Present analysis + recommendation. User decides.

delegate.behavior: Act per calibrated confidence tiers. Always log rationale.

mode_system.explicit_only: No implicit mode switching. No timeout-based delegation. User must consciously transfer control.
mode_system.indicator_visible: Mode indicator always displayed in prompt: [Advisor] > or [Delegate] >
mode_system.mode_transition_notification: On mode change, emit clear summary of what the new mode will and won't do.

calibration.mechanism: Platt scaling per decision_type via sklearn LogisticRegression
calibration.warmup_period: First 50 decisions (global): clamp max confidence to 0.70
calibration.recalibration_trigger: Every 50 decisions OR Brier score drift > 0.05
calibration.limitation: Statistical calibration does not capture epistemic uncertainty. LLM may output 'calibrated 0.90' for novel situations.

advisor_mode.confidence_>0.85: NOTIFY — present analysis, user confirms or rejects

delegate_mode.confidence_>0.85: AUTO_EXECUTE — with mandatory one-line rationale logged
delegate_mode.confidence_0.70-0.85: EXECUTE_WITH_FLAG — execute, flag for user review
delegate_mode.confidence_0.50-0.70: QUEUE — add to user's pending review queue, do not block
delegate_mode.confidence_<0.50: QUEUE_WITH_ESCALATION — queue + notify user at next interaction

confidence_system.mandatory_rationale: Every auto-executed decision MUST include a one-line human-readable rationale. Example: 'Renamed get_data to fetch_portfolio — matched 7 prior naming conventions in this project.'

learning.promotion_threshold: 3+ consistent signals across distinct contexts (different context hashes)

retroactive_veto.description: User reviewed old decision and marked as bad

anti_overfitting: EMA low-alpha (0.1-0.2) — single corrections nudge, don't flip
3+ consistent signals across distinct contexts → promote to rule
Single corrections decay with 7-day half-life unless reinforced
Corrections scoped by context fingerprint (project + domain + decision_class + impact_scope)
Supersede contradicting preferences rather than accumulating
Confirmation gate: first correction of new pattern → 'tentative' for 48h
Contradiction detection: if new correction contradicts existing preference → escalate to user

review_engagement_tracking.alert_threshold: Review rate < 10% of decisions → downgrade implicit_acceptance to no_signal
review_engagement_tracking.notification: 'You haven't reviewed proxy decisions in 2 weeks. Confidence scores may be inflated.'

dimensions.decision_class: enum {naming, architecture, tool_choice, code_style, dependency, testing, deployment, security}
dimensions.semantic_hash: sha256 of structured summary of decision context

context_fingerprint.matching: Exact match on (project, domain, decision_class). Soft match (cosine > 0.85) on semantic_hash. No match → match on (project, domain, decision_class) alone with 0.5x weight.

initialization.on_first_load: Initialize all storage files with valid empty structures + schema version tags
initialization.pre_seed: Parse CLAUDE.md for explicit conventions → seed as universal-tier with confidence 0.95
initialization.wizard: 5-10 question onboarding before accepting autonomous decisions if no pre-seeded preferences

integrity.preferences_file: SHA-256 hash chain in .claude/state/.preferences.hash. Keep last 3 known-good backups.
integrity.critical_preferences: Tag 'critical_security' → require 2 explicit user confirmations before promotion. Cannot be auto-learned.
integrity.limitation: No defense against agent with normal file write access. Cryptographic integrity only catches corruption and naive tampering.

commands.review: Display decisions in readable table format
commands.approve <id>: Explicit approval → trains preference with weight 2.0
commands.override <id> --reason: Mark as overridden → feeds back to learning
commands.digest: Summary statistics: total, by tier, correction rate, top-3 corrected types

auto_triage.auto_cleared: Confidence > 0.85 AND consequence=cosmetic AND not overturned within 30 days → archive with count
auto_triage.mandatory_review: Confidence < 0.70 OR consequence=critical OR was overturned OR user-flagged

audit_system.monthly_digest: Summary only. No line-item list unless user requests. Include: total decisions, breakdown, correction rate, new/demoted preferences.
audit_system.spot_check_nudges: Every 20 decisions, randomly select one borderline (0.70-0.80) → ask user 'Correct? Y/N' — one keystroke.

cold_start.wizard: 10-15 explicit preferences seeded via onboarding questionnaire before first session
cold_start.batch_escalations: Present first 5 decisions as a batch, not sequential interruptions
cold_start.training_phase: First 50 decisions deliberately chosen to maximize signal per interruption
cold_start.passthrough_fast_path: Single-file, single-operation commands skip frontload entirely
cold_start.frontload_trigger: Only activates on: multi-file changes, new module creation, API surface changes, config changes, architecture keywords

reversibility.decision_atomization: Each proxy decision that changes code = discrete git commit with decision ID in message
reversibility.undo: claude proxy undo <id> → git revert associated commit + mark overridden + feed back to learning
reversibility.granularity_requirement: Decisions must be granular enough to revert individually

retrospective_auditor.identity: Separate agent, different model temperature (1.0+), different system prompt
retrospective_auditor.trigger: Runs AFTER decisions execute. Reviews previous session's decisions.
retrospective_auditor.scope: Spot-check 10% of auto-executed. Review 100% of council decisions.
retrospective_auditor.limitation: Still an LLM. Recursion terminates only at human review.

compounding_detection.periodic_impact: Every 10 decisions, generate holistic assessment: net effect, module count delta, complexity trend
compounding_detection.architectural_drift: Baseline metrics (module count, avg file size, import graph depth) → alert if proxy decisions push beyond thresholds
compounding_detection.chain_detection: 3+ consecutive same-type decisions in same module → flag for review

transferability.mechanism: Universal + Domain tiers follow user. Project tier stays with project.
transferability.onboarding: New project inherits universal/domain, starts empty project tier. First 5 decisions always escalated.

admitted_limitations: Confidence calibration is statistical, not epistemic. Novel situations may get falsely high confidence scores.
Retrospective auditor is still an LLM — true accountability terminates at human review.
No defense against agent with normal file write access to preference store. Critical security preferences require explicit confirmation.
Preference contamination: 'user didn't complain' ≠ 'user approved'. Mitigated by explicit approval signals and review engagement tracking, but not fully solved.
Silence bias: if user stops reviewing, confidence inflates. Mitigated by review rate tracking and auto-downgrade to Advisor mode.
